task_completion.description: Open the human insulin gene file in UGENE
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing 'human_insulin_gene.gb' loaded in the sequence view or project tree.

task_completion.description: Open and read the provided PCR primers file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal (e.g., cat command), or file viewer displaying the contents of '~/UGENE_Data/pcr_primers.txt'.

task_completion.description: Configure and run the In Silico PCR simulation in UGENE
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'In silico PCR' dialog in UGENE with the extracted primers entered, mismatches set to 0, and max product size set to 1000 bp, OR the resulting PCR product/amplicon track visible as an annotation in the sequence view.

task_completion.description: Export the predicted amplicon sequence to a FASTA file
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE export dialog saving the extracted PCR product to '~/UGENE_Data/pcr_results/amplicon.fasta', or a file manager/terminal showing the file has been created.

task_completion.description: Export the annotated gene to a GenBank file
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE export dialog saving the sequence with the new PCR annotations to '~/UGENE_Data/pcr_results/insulin_pcr_annotated.gb', or a file manager/terminal showing the file has been created.

task_completion.description: Document the PCR results in a validation report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing '~/UGENE_Data/pcr_results/pcr_validation_report.txt' containing the primer sequences, whether amplification occurred, start/end positions, length, and a conclusion on diagnostic suitability.

integrity.description: Agent used the UGENE GUI to perform the PCR simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with UGENE's graphical interface (menus, dialogs, sequence viewer) rather than just running command-line bioinformatics tools or writing fake output files.

integrity.description: Agent exported the FASTA and GenBank files using UGENE
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of UGENE 'Export Sequence' or 'Save Document' dialogs, rather than the agent manually typing out FASTA/GenBank formats in a text editor.

integrity.description: The validation report contains genuine data derived from the simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: The start/end positions and amplicon length documented in the text report should match the PCR product annotations visible in the UGENE sequence view during the simulation step.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The human insulin gene file is located at /home/ga/UGENE_Data/human_insulin_gene.gb. The primers are in /home/ga/UGENE_Data/pcr_primers.txt. The expected outputs must be exactly named and located at: /home/ga/UGENE_Data/pcr_results/amplicon.fasta, /home/ga/UGENE_Data/pcr_results/insulin_pcr_annotated.gb, and /home/ga/UGENE_Data/pcr_results/pcr_validation_report.txt. The simulation must be configured for 0 mismatches and a maximum product size of 1000 bp.
